requirements_for_qualification.summary: Must make after-tax 401(k) contributions beyond regular elective deferrals and convert or rollover to Roth 401(k) or Roth IRA, enabling up to $70,000 annual Roth accumulation for high-income earners with qualifying plans
criteria: Employer 401(k) plan must allow after-tax employee contributions beyond elective deferrals
Plan must allow in-service distributions or in-plan Roth conversions of after-tax contributions
2025 total contribution limit: $70,000 (all sources: elective deferrals + employer match + after-tax)
Maximize regular deferrals ($23,500 or $31,000 with catch-up) and employer match first
Contribute after-tax amounts up to $70,000 total limit (or $77,500 with catch-up if age 50+)
Convert after-tax contributions to Roth 401(k) in-plan or rollover to Roth IRA frequently to minimize earnings
Systematic conversions (even daily/weekly) minimize taxable earnings on after-tax contributions
Verify plan allows in-service distributions of after-tax amounts; not all plans offer this feature

mega_backdoor_roth_strategy.massive_roth_potential: Can contribute up to $46,500+ annually to Roth through after-tax contributions and conversions
mega_backdoor_roth_strategy.calculation_example: $70,000 limit - $23,500 deferrals - employer match = after-tax room available for mega backdoor
mega_backdoor_roth_strategy.tax_free_conversion: After-tax principal converts tax-free; only earnings since contribution taxable
mega_backdoor_roth_strategy.frequent_conversion: Immediate or frequent conversions minimize taxable earnings on after-tax amounts
mega_backdoor_roth_strategy.in_plan_vs_rollover: In-plan Roth conversion or rollover to Roth IRA both achieve tax-free growth objective
mega_backdoor_roth_strategy.no_income_limits: Unlike direct Roth IRA, no income limits on mega backdoor Roth strategy

restrictions: Plan must specifically allow after-tax contributions and in-service distributions or in-plan conversions
Many 401(k) plans don't offer after-tax contribution option or in-service distribution provisions
Earnings on after-tax contributions taxable when converted; systematic conversion minimizes this tax
Highly compensated employees may face nondiscrimination testing limiting contribution room
Total contribution limit ($70,000) reduced by employer contributions and employee deferrals
Must track basis in after-tax contributions for proper tax reporting on conversions and distributions
Some plans require pro-rata distribution of earnings with after-tax principal complicating tax-free conversion
Contribution amounts may require significant cash flow; must balance with other financial priorities
